_comment: Resident Evil franchise data file. Includes all mainline games, spin-offs, films, series, books, and related media.
description: Capcom's survival horror juggernaut, debuting in 1996. Defined the genre with tense exploration, limited resources, and macabre biological horror. Evolved from fixed-camera angles to over-the-shoulder action, while spanning films, CGI features, novels, and an expanding universe of bioterrorism.

media.title: Resident Evil
media.description: The original survival horror classic. S.T.A.R.S. investigates the Arklay Mountains mansion, uncovering Umbrella's viral outbreak.
media.notes: Original version. Fixed camera angles, live-action intro. Re-released on Sega Saturn and DS.

media.title: Resident Evil 2
media.description: Raccoon City outbreak. Leon S. Kennedy and Claire Redfield's parallel scenarios define the series' A/B system.
media.notes: Set two months after RE1. Iconic zapping system, HUNK's 4th Survivor.

media.title: Resident Evil 3: Nemesis
media.description: Jill Valentine's escape from Raccoon City, pursued by the relentless Nemesis. Introduced live selection and ammo crafting.
media.notes: Originally titled Resident Evil 3: Last Escape in Japan. Nemesis is a series-defining stalker enemy.

media.title: Resident Evil Survivor
media.description: First-person light gun shooter. Ark Thompson investigates a Sheena Island outbreak, suffering from amnesia.
media.notes: Spin-off. Considered non-canon by many; references to Umbrella's early work.

media.title: Resident Evil Gaiden
media.description: Game Boy Color RPG-style adventure. Barry Burton and Leon investigate a new outbreak on the Starlight.
media.notes: Non-canon. Developed by M4, not Capcom. Rare and historically interesting.

media.title: Resident Evil Code: Veronica
media.description: Claire Redfield captured on Rockfort Island, discovers the Ashford family's legacy. Fully 3D environments.
media.notes: Originally Dreamcast exclusive. Considered mainline by fans; bridges RE2 and RE4. Later HD rereleases.

media.title: Resident Evil Zero
media.description: Prequel to RE1. Rebecca Chambers and convicted criminal Billy Coen escape a derailed train, fight the leech-man Marcus.
media.notes: No item boxes — drop items anywhere. HD remaster in 2016.

media.title: Resident Evil (2002 Remake)
media.description: The definitive version of the original. Crimson Heads, Lisa Trevor, expanded mansion. Still survival horror peak.
media.notes: GameCube exclusive originally. HD remaster in 2015.

media.title: Resident Evil Outbreak
media.description: Online-focused spin-off set during Raccoon City's fall. Eight civilians with unique abilities navigate separate scenarios.
media.notes: Pioneering online console game. Servers shut down, but playable offline.

media.title: Resident Evil Outbreak File #2
media.description: Second Outbreak collection. New scenarios like 'Wild Things' and 'Underbelly'. Refines original mechanics.
media.notes: Online play discontinued, but offline single-player remains.

media.title: Resident Evil 4
media.description: Revolutionary over-the-shoulder action. Leon Kennedy rescues the President's daughter from a cult in rural Spain. Las Plagas.
media.notes: Series-defining pivot. Countless ports, including a 2023 remake.

media.title: Resident Evil: The Umbrella Chronicles
media.description: Rail shooter summarizing events from RE0, RE1, RE2, RE3. Original chapters include 'Umbrella's End' and 'Dark Legacy'.
media.notes: Wii motion controls. PS3 version added Move support.

media.title: Resident Evil: The Darkside Chronicles
media.description: Rail shooter retelling RE2, RE:CV, and new story with Krauser in South America.
media.notes: More emotional, shaky-cam style. Co-op with Claire and Leon.

media.title: Resident Evil 5
media.description: Chris Redfield in Africa. Full co-op, more action-heavy. Final confrontation with Albert Wesker.
media.notes: Controversial at launch for action focus, but remains a top seller.

media.title: Resident Evil: The Mercenaries 3D
media.description: Mercenaries mode standalone. Score attack with RE4 and RE5 characters. No campaign.
media.notes: 3DS exclusive. Short-lived but functional.

media.title: Resident Evil: Revelations
media.description: Return to horror roots. Jill and Chris investigate the Queen Zenobia. Introduced episodic structure, scanning mechanic.
media.notes: Originally 3DS exclusive. HD ports widely available. Set between RE4 and RE5.

media.title: Resident Evil 6
media.description: Massive four-campaign action blockbuster. Leon, Chris, Jake, and Ada face C-virus global threat. Over-the-top setpieces.
media.notes: Divided fanbase. Later patches improved gameplay. Includes Mercenaries mode.

media.title: Resident Evil: Revelations 2
media.description: Claire Redfield and Barry Burton's daughter Moira on a remote island. Raid mode returns, episodic release.
media.notes: Ties into events before RE6. Raid mode highly replayable.

media.title: Resident Evil 7: Biohazard
media.description: First-person horror revival. Ethan Winters searches for his wife in the Baker family plantation. VR support. Full return to survival horror.
media.notes: RE Engine debut. Reinvented franchise. PS VR version exclusive for a year.

media.title: Resident Evil 2 (Remake)
media.description: Modern reimagining of RE2. Over-the-shoulder, Mr. X stalks, outstanding graphics. Critical and commercial peak.
media.notes: Set the template for RE Engine remakes. Separate scenarios streamlined but effective.

media.title: Resident Evil 3 (Remake)
media.description: Remake of RE3. Streamlined campaign, Nemesis redesigned, includes Resistance multiplayer mode.
media.notes: Shorter than RE2R, but polished. Resistance developed by NeoBards.

media.title: Resident Evil Village
media.description: Direct sequel to RE7. Ethan Winters explores a gothic village, confronting Lady Dimitrescu and Heisenberg. More action-oriented.
media.notes: Shadows of Rose DLC concludes the Winters arc. Mercenaries returns.

media.title: Resident Evil 4 (Remake)
media.description: Modern reimagining of RE4. Expanded narrative, streamlined combat, Separate Ways DLC fully rebuilt.
media.notes: Widely acclaimed as definitive RE4. Mercenaries and VR modes added post-launch.

media.title: Resident Evil: Resistance
media.description: Asymmetrical multiplayer 4v1. Survivors vs Mastermind, bundled with RE3R. Discontinued support.
media.notes: Standalone after RE3R launch. Servers remain active as of 2026.

media.title: Resident Evil Re:Verse
media.description: Multiplayer deathmatch with iconic characters. Bundled with RE Village. Short-lived player base.
media.notes: Released as standalone with Village. Service largely abandoned.

media.title: Resident Evil: Operation Raccoon City
media.description: Third-person shooter set during RE2/3 timeline. Play as Umbrella operatives rewriting history.
media.notes: Non-canon spin-off. Mixed reception.

media.title: Umbrella Corps
media.description: Competitive tactical shooter set in RE universe. Barebones, quickly abandoned.
media.notes: Universally panned. Quickest drop-off in series history.

media.title: Resident Evil (2002 Film)
media.description: First live-action film, directed by Paul W.S. Anderson. Milla Jovovich as Alice, a new character. The Hive beneath Raccoon City.
media.notes: Loose adaptation, launched six-film series. Financially successful but diverges from game canon.

media.title: Resident Evil: Apocalypse
media.description: Raccoon City outbreak. Alice allies with Jill Valentine and Carlos Oliveira against Nemesis.
media.notes: Introduces game characters Jill and Carlos.

media.title: Resident Evil: Extinction
media.description: Post-apocalyptic desert setting. Alice clones, Mad Max vibes. Claire Redfield appears.
media.notes: Transition to trilogy finale setup.

media.title: Resident Evil: Afterlife
media.description: Alice leads survivors against Umbrella. First in 3D. Chris Redfield introduced.
media.notes: Anderson returns to direct. High action, heavy slow-mo.

media.title: Resident Evil: Retribution
media.description: Alice captured in Umbrella facility, re-creates past locations. Ada Wong and Leon appear.
media.notes: Reverse-motion intro sequence famous.

media.title: Resident Evil: The Final Chapter
media.description: Alice returns to Raccoon City, confronts the Red Queen. Ends the Anderson film saga.
media.notes: Contradicts earlier continuity, but final box office success.

media.title: Resident Evil: Welcome to Raccoon City
media.description: Reboot film, faithful to games. Combines RE1 and RE2 timelines. Claire, Leon, Chris, Jill all appear.
media.notes: More faithful tone but mixed critical reception. Directed by Johannes Roberts.

media.title: Resident Evil: Degeneration
media.description: First CGI film. Leon and Claire reunite at an airport outbreak. Canon to games.
media.notes: Set between RE4 and RE5. Produced by Capcom and Sony Pictures.

media.title: Resident Evil: Damnation
media.description: CGI film. Leon in Eastern Europe, investigates bioweapons and Lickers used in civil war.
media.notes: Set between RE5 and RE6. Ada Wong features.

media.title: Resident Evil: Vendetta
media.description: CGI film. Chris, Leon, and Rebecca team up against a vengeance-fueled former Umbrella researcher.
media.notes: Features high-octane action, realistic CG. Set before RE7.

media.title: Resident Evil: Infinite Darkness
media.description: CGI limited series. Leon and Claire investigate a White House conspiracy and a new virus outbreak.
media.notes: CGI from the film team. Canon to games, set between RE4 and RE5.

media.title: Resident Evil (2022 Series)
media.description: Live-action series set across two timelines. Albert Wesker's daughters in New Raccoon City. Canceled after one season.
media.notes: Non-canon, diverges heavily. Canceled in 2022.

media.title: Resident Evil: The Umbrella Conspiracy (S.D. Perry)
media.description: First in S.D. Perry's novelizations. Adapts RE1 with expanded lore and character details.
media.notes: Seven-book series covering RE1 to RE:CV. Widely considered the definitive novelization.

media.title: Resident Evil: Caliban Cove (S.D. Perry)
media.description: Original novel. Rebecca teams with new characters in Maine against a new bio-weapon.
media.notes: Expands universe outside game canon.

media.title: Resident Evil: City of the Dead (S.D. Perry)
media.description: Novelization of RE2 and RE3 timelines. Follows Leon, Claire, and Jill.
media.notes: Combines events of both games.

media.title: Resident Evil: Underworld (S.D. Perry)
media.description: Original novel. Claire and the group infiltrate an Umbrella underground lab.
media.notes: Original narrative, set after RE3 events.

media.title: Resident Evil: Nemesis (S.D. Perry)
media.description: Novelization of RE3. Focuses on Jill's escape from Nemesis.
media.notes: Fleshes out the psychological torment of the pursuit.

media.title: Resident Evil: Code Veronica (S.D. Perry)
media.description: Novelization of Code: Veronica. Expands the Ashford family backstory.
media.notes: Final S.D. Perry novel. Series ended before RE4.

media.title: Resident Evil: Zero Hour (S.D. Perry)
media.description: Prequel novel to the novel series. Adapts events of RE0.
media.notes: Published after the main seven, bridges to earlier novels.

media.title: Resident Evil: The Marhawa Desire
media.description: Manga prequel to RE6. Set at a private school in Singapore with a new outbreak. Piers Nivans appears.
media.notes: Published in Weekly Shonen Champion. Canon to RE6 timeline.

media.title: Resident Evil: Heavenly Island
media.description: Manga set between RE5 and RE6. Claire investigates a tropical island with B.O.W.s and a tragic cult.
media.notes: Published in Monthly Shonen Sirius. Canon.

media.title: Resident Evil: The Official Comic Book (WildStorm)
media.description: WildStorm comic series. Adapts RE2 and original stories. Short run.
media.notes: Western comic, non-canon.

media.title: Biohazard 4: Incubate (Dengeki Comic)
media.description: Japanese anthology comic adapting RE4 side stories. Short-form.
media.notes: Rare, never officially translated.

media.title: Resident Evil: The Board Game (Steamforged)
media.description: Steamforged Games board game adaption of RE1, RE2, RE3. Campaign-style survival horror on tabletop.
media.notes: Crowdfunded, substantial miniatures and expansions.

media.title: Resident Evil: Deck Building Game
media.description: Bandai card game based on RE5 and earlier titles. Cooperative and competitive modes.
media.notes: Out of print. Includes characters and enemies from RE0–RE5.

media.title: Resident Evil: The Umbrella Chronicles (Artbook/Drama CD)
media.description: Japanese drama CD included with limited editions of Umbrella Chronicles. Audio narrative expanding the game's story.
media.notes: Rare, never localized. Short-form audio drama.
